methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $237M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.4B - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Saronic
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series D), post-money $9.25B

private.name: Chaos Industries
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series D), post-money $4.5B

data.narrative:
Primer AI is a Series D-stage defense and security software company that has raised $237M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 3 same-stage, same-sector comparables (Saronic, Chaos Industries, and Epirus), alongside a broader comp universe spanning 2 public companies and 8 private peers in the defense technology space.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 1.5x multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yielding $356M. Peer-median analysis across the three same-stage Defense & Security comparables produces a $4.5B implied valuation; notably, this peer set exhibits significant dispersion, ranging from $1.4B (Epirus) to $9.2B (Saronic).

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $302M (low) to $5.2B (high), with a median of $2.4B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity within the peer set and structural uncertainty about where Primer AI positions relative to more mature ($9.2B) versus earlier-stage ($1.4B) comparables. Key uncertainties include product maturity, customer concentration, defensibility of AI-driven defense analytics, and the path to sustained unit economics. The stage-progression method alone would suggest the lower bound; peer-median anchors the central estimate.
